data.title: Alpha Metallurgical misses Q3 top-line estimates; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Alpha Metallurgical press release (NYSE:AMR): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.16 in-line.
  • Revenue of $617.35M (-35.7% Y/Y) misses by $40.85M.
  • Posts Adjusted EBITDA of $53.2 million for the quarter.
  • Reduced letters of credit outstanding by $15 million during the quarter.

  • 2025 Guidance Adjustments and Performance Update

    Alpha is reducing its full-year 2025 guidance for metallurgical coal shipment volumes. The new metallurgical coal shipments guidance range is 14.5 million tons to 15.5 million tons, down from the prior range of 15.0 million tons to 16.0 million tons. Additionally, the company is increasing its 2025 cost of coal sales guidance range to $103.00 to $110.00 per ton, up from the prior range of $103.00 to $108.00 per ton.

    As of February 20, 2025, Alpha has committed and priced approximately 32% of its metallurgical coal for 2025 at an average price of $143.81 per ton and 95% of its thermal coal for the year at an average price of $80.74 per ton.

data.title: FuboTV Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.02 beats by $0.09, revenue of $431.82M misses by $13.35M
data.description:
• FuboTV press release (NYSE:FUBO): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.02 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $431.82M (+7.8% Y/Y) misses by $13.35M.
  • Average revenue per user in the fourth quarter was $87.90 in the region, an all-time high for the Company and an expansion of 1.4% YoY.

  • Guidance

    North America

    First Quarter 2025: Fubo is projecting $400 million to $410 million total revenue, representing 3% YoY growth at the midpoint, and 1.430 million to 1.460 million total subscribers, representing a 4% YoY decline at the midpoint.

  • ROW

    First Quarter 2025: Fubo is projecting $7.5 million to $8.5 million total revenue, representing a 5% YoY decline at the midpoint, and 330,000 to 340,000 subscribers, representing a 16% YoY decline at the midpoint.

data.title: Olam Group reports 2H results

  • Revenue of S$29.2B (+24% Y/Y).

data.title: Meta to release standalone AI app: report
data.description:
• Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) is planning on releasing a standalone app focusing on its artificial intelligence chatbot in an effort to compete with the likes of Microsoft (MSFT) backed OpenAI, Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini and others, CNBC reported.
  • The app is expected to debut in the second quarter, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. It will be a part of Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg's plan to make the company a leader in AI before the year is out.
  • In addition, Meta is planning to test a paid subscription service for Meta AI, similar to ChatGPT and Gemini and other generative AI chatbot apps.
  • Meta did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • In recent months, several tech companies have launched standalone generative AI apps in an effort to better build awareness and capitalize on the trend. Google launched a standalone Gemini app on iOS in November, while xAI, the AI company led by Elon Musk, launched a standalone iOS Grok app last month.
  • ChatGPT, which took the world by storm in November 2022, is routinely among the top free apps on the App Store and Google Play.

data.title: Old Republic raises dividend by 9.4%
data.description:
• Old Republic (NYSE:ORI) declared $0.29/share quarterly dividend, 9.4% increase from prior dividend of $0.27.
  • Forward yield 3.07%
  • Payable March 20; for shareholders of record March 10; ex-div March 10.
  • See ORI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Pfizer is latest Big Pharma to overhaul DEI goals after GSK
data.description:
Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) has made significant updates to its diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) webpage, becoming the latest Big Pharma after GSK (NYSE:GSK) to revise DEI initiatives amid the Trump administration’s moves to discourage diversity programs.

The U.S. pharma major has now titled the webpage “Merit-Based Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion,” revising the former title “Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion.”

According to Stat News, which first reported the news, Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) has also made significant changes to the webpage’s content.

“Our culture of diversity, equity, and inclusion is based on merit—one where hard work, talent, and contributions drive success, and barriers to opportunity are removed,” the New York-based company wrote, underscoring a merit-based culture.

Additionally, Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) has removed references to a racial equity assessment it commissioned in 2023 through a third-party contractor.

Meanwhile, in its annual report published on Thursday, British drugmaker GSK (NYSE:GSK) said it would no longer set "aspirational targets"—in reference to DEI initiatives—for leadership and supplier programs.

"We have paused, and in some cases, stopped certain initiatives to ensure we remain compliant with the law in the countries in which we operate, including the United States," the company said.

Pfizer (PFE) and GSK (GSK) are among the major U.S. and overseas corporations to review their DEI efforts after President Trump signed an executive order on his first day in office targeting equity-related programs at the federal level.

data.title: MPLX to buy remaining 55% stake in BANGL pipeline system in $715M deal
data.description:
MPLX (NYSE:MPLX) said Friday it agreed to acquire the remaining 55% interest in the BANGL pipeline system in Texas from affiliates of WhiteWater and Diamondback Energy (FANG) for $715M; upon achievement of specific financial performance metrics, MPLX also would make earnout payments up to a specified cap.

The BANGL system transports up to 250K bbl/day of natural gas liquids from the Permian Basin to fractionation markets along the U.S. Gulf Coast, MPLX (NYSE:MPLX) said, adding that its own fractionation complex is expected to begin service in 2028; BANGL is being expanded to 300K bbl/day, which is anticipated to come online in H2 2026.

"With full ownership of BANGL and its expansion opportunities, our growth platform is further improved for the long term as we connect growing NGL production from the Permian Basin to our recently announced Gulf Coast fractionation complex," MPLX (MPLX) President and CEO Maryann Mannen said.

data.title: Akero Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.99 beats by $0.17
data.description:
• Akero Therapeutics press release (NASDAQ:AKRO): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.99 beats by $0.17.
  • Akero's cash, cash equivalents and short- and long-term marketable securities for the year ended December 31, 2024 were $797.8 million.
  • Akero believes that its cash, cash equivalents and short and long-term marketable securities will be sufficient to fund its current operating plan into 2028.

data.title: Apellis Pharmaceuticals GAAP EPS of -$0.29 beats by $0.10, revenue of $212.5M beats by $14.59M
data.description:
• Apellis Pharmaceuticals press release (NASDAQ:APLS): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.29 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $212.5M (+45.2% Y/Y) beats by $14.59M.
  • Total revenue was $212.5 million for the fourth quarter of 2024, which consisted of $167.8 million in U.S. net product revenue of SYFOVRE, $23.4 million in U.S. net product revenue of EMPAVELI and $21.4 million in licensing and other revenue associated with the Sobi collaboration.
